mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce a positive return frequency above 58% over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 during confirmed expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising wage income, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10) across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above the threshold consistently coincide with positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and rising retail sales validate aggregate demand momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative return frequency exceeding 50% during confirmed early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and falling corporate revenues, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production contractions fail to predict negative returns with a directional accuracy below 45% over 5-day horizons.
